OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a 71-year-old man living in Philadelphia, I've been involved in many conversations lately regarding the COVID vaccines. It's an emotional topic for me because my mother passed away from complications related to a previous illness when she was around my age. Although I work part-time as a sales associate and don't have much formal education, I feel anxious about these discussions so I try to approach them calmly. My Buddhist beliefs help me stay open-minded while being agreeable with others' perspectives.

I've found that the best way for someone like me to handle vaccine conversations is to first listen carefully without interrupting too quickly. Since my parents have advanced degrees but mine only has a vocational diploma, I don't feel confident making medical decisions myself. This makes me want to gather information from reliable sources before sharing an opinion.

Being highly extraverted means I enjoy social interactions, especially running and cycling with friends - these activities help reduce my anxiety during discussions. My high openness keeps me curious about new health developments but sometimes causes worry when misinformation spreads online or through word-of-mouth. I spend a few times weekly on social media which exposes me to many opinions.

So what's worked best for me is suggesting everyone consult their own healthcare provider, just like in Buddhism where personal insight varies with each individual. Then sharing my experience: how as an immigrant who has seen family members fall ill over the years, I understand why vaccination might be important but also want to protect vulnerable populations.

I think being open to learning while maintaining a low agreeableness level (around 20) helps me set boundaries if someone becomes too argumentative. My moderate neuroticism means I do feel anxiety sometimes during these conversations, especially when misinformation is shared online, so I remind myself that knowledge comes from many sources and we should all seek truth.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I started with assessing my personal background (age, education, anxiety), then connected it to my Buddhist beliefs. Next, I considered how my extraversion helps but also makes me anxious in social situations. Then discussed the influence of being an immigrant on healthcare views. Finally addressed the low agreeableness trait by talking about setting boundaries.
response_parsed.tone: Calm yet engaged with occasional anxiety
